title: AlpaSim closed-loop real-time performance on the A40 (tanitad-eval) — MEASURED 2026-07-23
device: NVIDIA A40 (46 GB), single GPU shared by renderer + model
_evidence: isolated ticks: rt_model_iso.json (rt_model_iso.py, no renderer contention). in-situ loop: rt_timing.json (rt_driver.py instrumented, flagship, 480x854). native RT factor: RUN_RECIPE s14 (10.04 sim-s / 34.5 wall).

verdict.is_real_time: resolution-dependent: NEAR real-time at 480x854 (~0.75-0.98x), SUB-real-time at native 1080x1920 (0.29x)
verdict.bottleneck: the RENDERER (NuRec gsplat, scales ~5x 854->native and drives the RT drop) PLUS a fixable driver CPU cost (frame re-canonicalization, ~475 ms at native). The MODEL GPU forward (~30-90 ms) is NOT the bottleneck.

in_situ_loop_480x854_model_driver.note: loop_gap = wall between consecutive drive() calls = the effective per-step throughput. p90 484 ms = renderer scene-load stalls. The constant (no-model) driver loop was NOT faster (median 270-350 ms, noisier) -> the model is NOT the gate; render/environment is.

isolated_component_ticks_ms_median._source: rt_model_iso.json (A40, no renderer contention; nframes_24 = steady-state deque, nframes_10 = minimum)

flagship_v1.note: 8-frame DINOv2 encode + strategic + tactical; RESOLUTION-INDEPENDENT (256x256 input). ~90 ms, NOT 28 ms.

refc_base.note: lighter anchored-diffusion (2 steps); ~18-40 ms. The ~28 ms estimate matches REF-C, not flagship.

frame_canon_cpu._what: ftheta_crop_resize + 3-frame stack of the frame history, on CPU, inside the driver plan()
frame_canon_cpu.FINDING: the driver RE-CANONICALIZES the WHOLE deque (up to 24 frames) EVERY step -> 475 ms of pure CPU preprocessing at native res, dwarfing the model. Caching already-canon'd frames would cut the driver plan to model-only (~30-90 ms). A real, fixable inefficiency.

isolated_component_ticks_ms_median.renderer_nurec: not isolated standalone (no standalone render client built); inferred as the dominant loop remainder. At native the per-step loop is ~690 ms; the driver plan (canon+model, pipelined) is ~500 ms steady; render+physics+IPC is the co-dominant remainder and scales ~5x with pixels (854->native).
isolated_component_ticks_ms_median.physics_ipc: small: localhost gRPC (~ms/call) + a light warp ground-mesh solve. Not isolated cleanly (constant-driver control was scene-load-noisy); folded into the loop remainder.

corrections_to_prior_estimate: The '~3 Hz / 0.3x' estimate holds at NATIVE 1080x1920 (0.29x) but NOT at 480x854, where it is ~0.75-0.98x (near the 5 Hz control rate) — the renderer cost scales ~5x with pixels.
The flagship model tick is ~90 ms (not ~28 ms). 28 ms ~ REF-C's lighter diffusion, or the GPU-forward measured uncontended. flagship caps at ~11 Hz on the model alone, not 35 Hz.
'renderer-bound' is right in spirit, but at native res a co-equal cost is the driver's redundant CPU frame-canon (~475 ms) — a software fix, not a GPU limit.
caveats: In-situ loop numbers are confounded by (a) GPU CONTENTION — renderer + model share one A40 — and (b) gRPC CONCURRENCY (driver ThreadPool max_workers=8 overlaps drive() calls, so in-situ model_infer 396 ms > loop_gap 205 ms), and (c) renderer SCENE-LOAD stalls (BackendCache maxsize=5; multi-second p90). The ISOLATED ticks (rt_model_iso) are clean; the in-situ loop_gap is the honest effective throughput.
A clean render-vs-physics split was not obtained (the constant-driver control was dominated by scene-load stalls, n low). render+physics+IPC reported as one bucket; render dominates it.
480x854 measured this session; native 1080x1920 RT factor from RUN_RECIPE s14 (single scene, clean 0.29x).
